title: Madronella dentata
taxonomy: Madronella dentata is a member of the family Calyceraceae, which is a small family of flowering plants native to South America. It belongs to the order Asterales and is closely related to other members of the asterid clade. The genus Madronella is part of the Calyceraceae family and is primarily found in Chile and Argentina. This plant classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic analysis. Further research may be needed to confirm its exact classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Madronella dentata, also known as the Chilean rose, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
